description: Migration: create `opportunities` table (tenant-scoped) with number, client_id, contact_id, title, type, owner_id, status, derived stage, confidence, mrr_cents/nrr_cents/hardware_cents + currency_code, expected_close_date, next_action + next_action_due, last_activity_at, loss_reason/lost_to, generator provenance (key + context jsonb), converted contract/project refs
Migration: create `opportunity_evidence` table (checkpoint, source system|declared, ref_type/ref_id, recorded_by/at, correction audit note)

Migration: create `opportunity_suggestions` table (generator_key, client_id, evidence payload jsonb, computed values, status pending|accepted|dismissed|snoozed, snooze_until, dedupe_key unique per tenant+generator, created opportunity_id)
Migration: add `clients.lifecycle_status` enum (prospect|active|former) with backfill of all existing rows to active
Migration: add nullable `interactions.opportunity_id` (FK) so interactions serve as the opportunity timeline
Migration: add FK constraint `quotes.opportunity_id` → opportunities (verify all existing values NULL first)
Migration: register next_number entity OPPORTUNITY with prefix OPP- for opportunity numbering
Migration: seed RBAC permission rows for resource `opportunities` (create/read/update/delete, MSP portal only)
Extend TaggedEntityType with `opportunity` and wire tag support (definitions/mappings) for opportunities

Migration: seed event catalog with OPPORTUNITY_CREATED, OPPORTUNITY_STAGE_CHANGED, OPPORTUNITY_STATUS_CHANGED, OPPORTUNITY_STALLED, OPPORTUNITY_ESCALATED, OPPORTUNITY_NEXT_ACTION_OVERDUE, OPPORTUNITY_SUGGESTION_CREATED under the CRM category
TypeScript interfaces in packages/types: IOpportunity, IOpportunityEvidence, IOpportunitySuggestion, stage/checkpoint/status/confidence enums, list-item and detail view models
Zod schemas for opportunity create/update/list filters/suggestion actions in packages/opportunities/src/schemas
Scaffold packages/opportunities (models/actions/schemas/components/index per house pattern) and register msp-composition provider
Add top-level Opportunities nav item in menuConfig.ts routing to /msp/opportunities
Register /msp/opportunities route rules in productSurfaceRegistry: PSA allowed, AlgaDesk upgrade_boundary; API prefix rules for /api/v1/opportunities
PostHog feature flag gating the Opportunities nav item for soft launch
Opportunity model (tenant-scoped knex CRUD with pagination/filter query helpers, no N+1 value rollups)
createOpportunity action: validates client, assigns OPP- number, defaults owner to client account manager else creator, rejects creation without next_action + next_action_due
updateOpportunity action (title, contact, type, expected_close_date, owner, values pre-quote, confidence) with permission enforcement
Win action: sets status won + won_at, records Won evidence, transitions prospect client lifecycle to active, emits OPPORTUNITY_STATUS_CHANGED and CLIENT_STATUS_CHANGED
Lose action: requires loss_reason (enum + optional text and lost_to), sets lost_at, emits OPPORTUNITY_STATUS_CHANGED
List/filter action: server-side pagination and filters by status, stage, owner, client, type, staleness
getOpportunity action returning detail view model: evidence ladder, values, linked quotes, timeline summary, tags
Evidence recording API: record checkpoint fact with source (system|declared) and entity ref; idempotent per checkpoint+ref
Stage derivation engine: stage = furthest checkpoint reached with skippable checkpoints (renewal/expansion deals enter mid-ladder); recomputed on evidence change; emits OPPORTUNITY_STAGE_CHANGED
Declared Qualified checkpoint action (decision-maker + budget conversation attestation, the one human-attested checkpoint)
Evidence correction flow: evidence is append-only; corrections require an audit note; no deletion path exists
Quote sent event records Proposed evidence on the linked opportunity automatically
Quote accepted / contract sent-for-signature records Verbal evidence on the linked opportunity automatically
Assessment evidence: accepted quote (or created project) matching the tenant's assessment mapping records Assessment checkpoint; assessment detection configured in module settings
Declared confidence field (low|medium|high|committed) settable independently of stage; never alters derived stage
Manual MRR/NRR/hardware value entry with tenant currency handling (standard currency components, cents storage)
Value derivation from linked accepted quote lines: is_recurring → MRR, product kind → hardware, else NRR
Value recompute on quote item/status change; manual values allowed only before a quote is linked (override rules)
Create quote from opportunity: prefills client/contact and sets quotes.opportunity_id
Link/unlink an existing quote to an opportunity (with evidence and value recompute side effects)
Won flow guided conversion step: accepted quote → draft agreement via existing conversion; store converted contract ref on the opportunity
Won flow optional project spawn: pick a project template, create onboarding project, store project ref on the opportunity
CLIENT_STATUS_CHANGED domain event builder + emission for all lifecycle_status transitions (the catalogued event becomes real)
Won-without-quote path: manual values accepted, reduced evidence recorded, no conversion step required
Quick-add prospect client (name-only) inline from opportunity creation; creates client with lifecycle_status=prospect
lifecycle_status surfaced on client create/edit forms and client schemas
Operational client lists filter prospects out by default with an explicit filter to include them
Client detail gains an Opportunities tab listing the client's opportunities with create shortcut
Prospect clients visually distinct (badge) wherever client names render in opportunity surfaces
/msp/opportunities page shell with tabs: Queue (default), Pipeline, Board, Suggestions (+ EE tabs when licensed)
Pipeline list view: title, client, stage, confidence, MRR/NRR, next action due, owner; server-side sort/filter/pagination; no raw IDs
Opportunity detail screen: header with values and status actions, evidence ladder visualization with source refs, confidence control, linked quotes section, tags
Create/edit opportunity form (standard components; client picker with quick-add prospect; next action + due required)
Board view: stage columns Identified→Verbal plus recent Won/Lost rail; cards show MRR/NRR, staleness badge, next-action due
Board drag rules: forward drag only onto declared checkpoints (Qualified); drag to Lost prompts loss reason; evidence-derived positions are otherwise not draggable
Opportunity timeline: log/list interactions from the opportunity detail (reusing interactions module UI), linked via interactions.opportunity_id
REST API v1: /api/v1/opportunities CRUD (controller + service + schemas) following the quotes API pattern
REST API v1: suggestion endpoints (list, accept, dismiss, snooze) and evidence endpoints (list, record declared)
OpenAPI registration for all opportunity endpoints
Permission enforcement on all opportunity server actions and API routes (opportunities resource, action-level checks)
Opportunity domain event builders (shared/workflow/streams/domainEventBuilders) publishing all OPPORTUNITY_* events via the standard helpers
Complete-next-action flow: completing an action immediately requires setting the next action + due date (the chain never breaks while status=open)
last_activity_at maintenance: updated by action completion, logged interactions, and quote lifecycle events on the deal
Staleness detection job: scheduled scan flags open opportunities silent past the nudge threshold (default 14d) and emits OPPORTUNITY_STALLED
Nudge notification: private notification (in-app + email template) to the opportunity owner listing the stalled deal and its last activity
Escalation at interrupt threshold (default 21d), solo mode: auto-create ad_hoc schedule entry calendar block ('Follow up: {opportunity}') on the owner's calendar; emits OPPORTUNITY_ESCALATED
Escalation, team mode: private notification chain (rep first, then account-manager/owner) — never a public surface
Module settings: tenant-configurable nudge/interrupt thresholds and solo-vs-team escalation mode
Work queue view: sections for overdue/due-today next actions, stalled deals, and pending suggestions; celebratory empty state
Queue one-click interactions: complete action (with inline next-action prompt), log interaction, snooze, mark lost — sub-30-second deal touch
OPPORTUNITY_NEXT_ACTION_OVERDUE emission when a next action passes its due date
User Activities dashboard integration: opportunity next actions appear as a new activity source in the aggregator
Weekly digest email (notification template): actions due, stalled deals, new suggestions, recent wins; per-user toggle
Default-on system workflows implementing the nudge/escalation methodology (registered so EE Designer can customize; CE runs the defaults)
Generator framework: generators produce suggestions (never opportunities), each with evidence payload, computed values, and a stable dedupe_key
Generator scheduling: recurring job runs plus on-demand 'run now' per generator from settings; run summary logging (fired/deduped counts)
Suggestions review UI: evidence payload rendered per generator type; accept / dismiss / snooze actions
Accept suggestion → creates opportunity prefilled with client, type, values, and generator provenance; suggestion links to created opportunity
Dismiss persists dedupe so the same suggestion does not refire; snooze re-surfaces at snooze_until
OPPORTUNITY_SUGGESTION_CREATED event emission per new suggestion
Renewal generator: fires at configurable T-days (default 120) before client_contract end/decision dates; suggestion linked 1:1 to the renewal work item; MRR prefilled from contract monthly value
Renewal-born opportunities typed renewal and enter the ladder mid-stream (Proposed when the renewal quote goes out)
T&M conversion generator: trailing-12-month hourly/T&M billed totals per client vs configurable monthly-average threshold
T&M evidence one-pager: printable client-facing view of monthly T&M spend history vs agreement comparison ('reporting, not selling')
Whitespace grid computation: service categories × active clients matrix derived from active contract lines
Whitespace grid UI (CE): full-book grid view; empty-cell click creates a whitespace suggestion for that client+service line
Asset aging generator: assets past the configured purchase/warranty date threshold grouped per client into refresh suggestions with asset-list evidence
Report definition: pipeline by stage (count, MRR, NRR, hardware) with owner/type dimensions
Report definition: win/loss outcomes with loss reasons and lost-to breakdown
Report definition: won rate among closed deals with an Assessment checkpoint compared with closed deals without one
Report definition: generator yield (suggestions → accepted → won, count and value, per generator)
MSP dashboard widget: pipeline snapshot (open count, MRR/NRR by stage) + queue summary (actions due, stalled count)
EE tier gating for all management-layer surfaces (tabs, endpoints, actions) via existing tier-gating seam; CE loses nothing from the core loop
Forecast band computation: floor = verbal + won this period; ceiling = evidence-weighted open pipeline; never a single number
Per-seller calibration feeding the ceiling: historical declared-confidence-vs-outcome rates per seller once sufficient closed-deal history exists, with evidence-stage base rates as fallback
Forecast UI: band with composition drill-down (which deals make the floor vs ceiling) per period
Calibration report: per seller declared confidence vs actual close rate
Attach rate report: per seller, share of won new-logo deals closing with an agreement attached
Realized effective rate vs quoted report (quoted line rates vs realized billing on converted agreements)
Pipeline meeting mode: full-screen deal-by-deal walk showing evidence-vs-declared gap, days silent, next action; keyboard-driven advance
Meeting session persistence: per-meeting reviewed markers per deal, resumable within the session
Migration (EE): `opportunity_commitments` table (description, made_by/at, resolution_status open|quote_line|agreement_line|project_task|declined, resolution ref, resolved_by/at)
Commitments CRUD on the opportunity detail (record promises as they're made)
Close gate: close-won is blocked while unresolved commitments exist (EE enforcement)
Commitment resolution linking: resolve each commitment to a quote line, agreement line, or project task ref, or explicitly decline it
Handoff visibility: spawned onboarding project surfaces the deal's commitments and courtship timeline to the delivery engineer
QBR trigger pack: per-account view assembling renewal dates, aging/EOL assets, ticket volume trend, and whitespace rows for AMs
QBR one-click 'Create opportunities?' from fired triggers (batch accept into typed opportunities)
QBR→pipeline tracking: opportunities created from QBR triggers attributed and reportable (triggers fired vs pipeline created per account/AM)
Rollups by seller and by office (per resolved attribution rule) across pipeline, wins, and attach metrics
Workflow actions registered with Zod schemas: opportunities.create, opportunities.find, opportunities.update, opportunities.set_next_action (Designer-usable with form metadata)
AI module seam gating: drafting features render/execute only when the tenant's AI module is enabled
Follow-up draft generation from opportunity context (timeline, stage, quote state, staleness) available from detail and queue
Draft editor + send through existing tenant outbound email; no send without explicit user action (no auto-send path exists)
Sent drafts logged as interactions on the opportunity and update last_activity_at
Per-user voice profile: pasted sample emails and/or steering instructions stored and editable in user settings
Voice profile applied to every draft; per-draft tone adjustment control

Why-sentence composer: fact-templating engine that assembles one-sentence explanations from structured evidence (day counts, linked-artifact refs, checkpoint states, historical ratios) for queue rows, suggestions, nudges, and the digest — no AI dependency, one bold clause per sentence

Queue lesson strip: library of own-numbers insight computations (e.g. assessment close rate vs. proposals this quarter) rendering one insight per visit with a single follow-up action, degrading gracefully on thin history
